repo_prior_art.cite: none; the only other PartialID catalogue entry is pid_manski1990test, with Manski mean-bound tightening

published_prior_art.cite: fan_guerre_zhu_2017_joint_po_functionals studies joint-potential-outcome functionals in latent threshold-crossing models, but does not state this discrete generalized-Roy common-law pasting lemma

repo_prior_art.cite: none; pid_manski1990test concerns bounded-outcome Manski intervals, not IV/OT policy gain-band sensitivity

published_prior_art.cite: tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot gives generalized-Roy PRTE OT bounds for product costs; Masten and Poirier's breakdown-frontier work is the closer omitted antecedent for a robustness-to-sign-reversal frontier
tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot supplies the separable/product-cost regime that this proposition merely nests

per_theorem_calls.label: Open-ended Question 1 (orthogonal inference for sign radius)

published_prior_art.cite: ober_reynolds_2023_joint_po_ot supplies root-n dual-OT endpoint inference and fang_santos_2019_directional_inference supplies the directional-delta framework; generated generalized-Roy cutpoints remain a distinct combination

structure_flags.label: Theorem 1
structure_flags.one_line: The proposed headline is an unproved open kernel but is labelled a theorem; reclassify it as a conjecture (or make the OEQ the explicit headline) before review.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The omitted Masten--Poirier breakdown-frontier literature is a closer published antecedent for the claimed sign-reversal sensitivity frontier; distinguish a genuinely new generalized-Roy result from ordinary parametric-LP re-expression.

novelty_flags.label: Bibliography / gao_ge_qian_2025_causal_mot
novelty_flags.one_line: The cited paper is not by Ruocheng Gao, Yuxuan Ge, and Yao Qian and is not titled 'Causal Partial Identification via Multi-Marginal Optimal Transport'; the AISTATS 2025 paper is Gao, Ge, and Qian, 'Bridging Multiple Worlds: Multi-marginal Optimal Transport for Causal Partial-identification Problem.'

novelty_flags.label: Bibliography / han_yang_2024_iv_bounds
novelty_flags.one_line: The claimed Han--Yang 2024 working paper title does not identify the verified paper: Sukjin Han and Shenshen Yang's relevant work is 'A Computational Approach to Identification of Treatment Effects for Policy Evaluation,' which gives sharp nonparametric policy/MTE bounds under IV independence.

soundness_flags.label: Lemma 1 and definitions of A_m, R_infty(P)
soundness_flags.one_line: The untreated factual-map coordinate is mis-indexed: [A_m r]_{0ℓi} sums r_{mkji}, hence the Y(1)=y_i marginal, whereas consistency with D=0 requires sum_j r_{mkij}, the Y(0)=y_i marginal; the stated Q-to-polytope implication is false even in the elementary threshold model.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: delta_L(P,epsilon) and delta_U(P,epsilon) are defined as min/max over R_epsilon(P) without a nonemptiness/compatibility domain or empty-set convention, although the theorem is stated as a functional of P.

soundness_flags.label: ass:adjacent-cutpoint-coverage
soundness_flags.one_line: Exact coverage of every cutpoint in every covariate cell is a strong finite-design saturation condition, not a generally standard 'full adjacent threshold support' assumption established by the cited generalized-Roy PRTE paper; retag and justify it or state the result conditionally as a deliberately discrete design regime.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 1 / technical_internal_limitation
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The identified set and sign boundary are conditional on finite grids, both monotone-rank restrictions, and the calibrated pooled rank-association floor; unrestricted dependence is not identified.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Sharp only under the displayed finite-grid generalized-Roy and rank-association sensitivity model.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Root-n inference, tie handling, and generated-cutpoint regularity are not established by the identification kernel.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The proposal's settled identification claim makes no inference guarantee; inference remains an explicit OEQ.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Correct the untreated block of A_m to sum_j r_{mkij}, then add a K=1/two-threshold hand calculation verifying both factual marginals and common-law pasting before making any sharpness claim.
Give delta_L and delta_U an explicit compatible-law domain (or an empty-feasible-set convention) and state exactly which observed P are in scope.

Replace the generic LP sign-radius novelty claim with a strict published comparator result: prove a nonseparable gain-band bound or calibrated rank-association restriction that is strictly tighter than the verified Han--Yang or Tan--Blanchet--Syrgkanis bounds on a named observable-law class, and compare it to the Masten--Poirier breakdown frontier.

Repair the Gao and Han--Yang citations, add Masten--Poirier to related work, and explain why the proposed frontier is not merely the standard feasibility reparameterization of a finite linear program.

recommended_next_step: Pivot back to Stage -1: repair the factual map first, then demonstrate a strict, non-routine comparator advance beyond existing breakdown-frontier and generalized-Roy LP/OT work before resubmission.
